description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from large, rectangular stone blocks, forming a robust and imposing structure. The front face features a prominent, deeply recessed archway with three concentric, stepped arches, each supported by a series of engaged columns or pilasters on either side. The archway sits atop a broad, flat base, and the entire structure is capped with a solid, rectangular entablature. The rear face is simpler, with a single arch outlined by stonework but lacking the ornate, layered arches and columns of the front. The stone surface is rough and matte, with visible block joints and a weathered, historical appearance. There are no visible decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels, giving the arch a utilitarian, Romanesque character.
description: A large, stone triumphal arch with a deeply recessed, multi-layered front archway and a simpler rear face, built from rectangular stone blocks. The structure is robust, with a rough, weathered surface and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main archway with three concentric arches and engaged columns or pilasters on each side, all set in a blocky stone facade.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the thickness of the structure and the block construction, with little detail visible beyond the stonework.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a single, plain arch outlined in stone, lacking the layered arches and columns of the front.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors the other side, showing the solid, blocky profile and the depth of the archway.
